one_time_description: Member distributions related to Williams Treaty and Islands in the Trent settlements totalling $10,607,191 recorded as expenses in Administration segment
economic_notes: First Nation operates Old Railroad Stop (gas bar and store). Council passed resolution to transfer $90,000,000 in investments to the Bijiinago, Noongom, Waabang Trust. COVID-19 impacted revenues from Old Railroad Stop, tent and trailer park, and restaurant rental. Operating loan of $16,555,000 obtained from TD Bank to finance Cultural Centre, L.I.F.E. Services Building expansion, Dnaagdawenmag Binnoojiiyag Child & Family Services Building, and land purchase.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Large settlement funds (Williams Treaty and Islands in the Trent) dominate the balance sheet with ~$97M in restricted investments. Operating loan of $16,555,000 classified as current liability (demand loan). First Nation was not in compliance with DSC covenant but TD Bank waived non-compliance. Council resolution to transfer $90M to external trust (Bijiinago, Noongom, Waabang Trust) is a major subsequent event. Member distributions of $10,607,191 recorded as expenses significantly distort the expense total. Restricted cash includes Ontario First Nations LP ($2,562,711), Lottery ($5,364), Williams Treaty settlement ($1,227,433), and Islands in the Trent settlement ($2,949). No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements provided. Annual debt service is $0 as the operating loan had no principal repayments during the year.
